title: Terrific broth (TB) medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cmc9i","text":"IBI’s Terrific Broth is used with Glycerol in cultivating recombinant strains of E. coli. Terrific broth is a highly enriched medium for improving yield in plasmid bearing E. coli. Recombinant strains have an extended growth phase in the medium. The addition of tryptone and yeast extract in the medium will allow higher plasmid yield per volume. Glycerol is used as a carbohydrate source in this formulation. Unlike glucose, glycerol is not fermented to acetic acid.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":81,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":172,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bduf3","text":"Follow step by step, unless stated otherwise. Equipment needed should be standard to a microbiology lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fj04n","text":"Prepare glassware by cleaning it, and ensure that scale is sufficiently calibrated","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9nrg","text":"You can mix Dextrose from the beginning with the other compounds, and autoclave together. While this is more time efficient and easier, it is important to take into account the possibility of toxic byproducts produced by the Millard reaction when autoclaving, producing Acrylamide, a probable human carcinogen (IARC Group 2A).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4k2f5","text":"Furthermore, when removing autoclaved components, be sure to take care as these can be very hot. If using antibiotics, use sufficient PPE to protect yourself, as some can be toxic to humans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8o8f0","text":"Analytical scale, autoclave, bottle(s), weight vessel, LAF bench","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill the bottle with 200 mL distilled water Consider making a larger batch of 5 bottles of base solution at once and use all Potassium phosphate solution and carbon solution at once to get 2.5 L of medium

Measure 11.8 g Yeast extract and 5.9 g Tryptone. Add 7.5 g for making agar platesMaterials: Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875 Tryptone Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #T9410 Agar Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A1296
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Add 100 mL Potassium phosphate buffer (1 M, 6.2 pH) Potassium phosphate buffer (1.0 M, pH 6.2) by Andreas Sagen
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 7.2 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add distilled water to a total of 400 mL
Fill the bottle with 300 mL double-distilled water
Add 20 mL Glycerol and mix solution well Materials: Glycerol MP Biomedicals Catalog #194680 (CAS: 56-81-5)
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 7.2 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add distilled water to a total of 500 mL
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00 Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00
Add 100 mL carbon solution to base solution
Aliquot medium in 100 mL units, and store refrigerated ( 4 °C ) If there is no space to store refrigerated, keep sterile and store at room temperature
creator: Andreas Sagen
